title: DataDelver
description: Exploring the labyrinth of Data Science, Machine Learning, and MLOps one delve at a time.

items.title: Delve 22: A Local Claude Code Redux
items.content_html: Hello data delvers! It's been some time but I'm back with more delves! In the time since my previous delve the landscape for local llm development has already shifted quite a bit so I want to come back with a refreshed setup guide!

items.title: Delve 10: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 4, Configuration
items.content_html: ML Microservices, The Great Env-scape Hello data delvers! In part three of this series we refactored our application into three separate layers, allowing us to better separate concerns within our codebase. However, if we examine certain parts of our code we can still observe some brittleness:

items.title: Delve 11: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 5, Testing
items.content_html: ML Microservices, Keep Calm and Run Your Tests Hello data delvers! In part four of this series we refactored our application to include a configuration file to make it easy to switch configuration values per development environment. In this part we'll cover a critical element to building scalable systems: Testing.

items.title: Delve 12: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 6, Containerization
items.content_html: ML Microservices, Deployment through Docker Hello data delvers! In part five of this series we added automated tests to our application to make it easier to catch bugs. In this part we'll cover packaging and deploying the app!

items.title: Delve 13: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 7, Model Tracking and APIs with MLFlow
items.content_html: Reset and Rescope Hello data delvers! In part six of this series we containerized our application, making it portable and easy to deploy. For this part we will take a step back. Introduce machine learning (finally!), and explore how we can begin to incorporate machine learning models into our microservice ecosystem!

items.title: Delve 15: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 8, The Orchestrator Service
items.content_html: Machine Learning Services as a System Greetings data delvers! In part seven of this series we finally deployed a model! For this part we'll examine how to utilize our model as part of a larger microservice ecosystem!

items.title: Delve 19: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 9, Docker Container Optimization
items.content_html: Greetings data delvers! In part eight of this series we deployed our first multi-service system. In this part, we examine more deeply how we are deploying our services with Docker and look for opportunities to make our deployment more optimized and secure.

items.title: Delve 23: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 10, Improving DevX with AI
items.content_html: Greetings data delvers! It has been a while since we last delved into modern ML microservices. In part nine of this series we looked at optimizing our docker container setup. Since then, AI of course has become a much more prominent tool in the software engineering space. While there has been much commentary about how...

items.title: Delve 4: The ML Engineer, Coming to an Enterprise Near You
items.content_html: Who am I? Hello data delvers! I hope your year is off to a good start! For this delve I wanted to cover a question that I get asked often, especially whenever I meet someone new, the dialog usually goes something like this:

items.title: Delve 6: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 1
items.content_html: A New Year, A New Start Hello data delvers! Happy New Year! I hope you all have been well! It's been some time since our last delve (sorry!) but I want to kick off 2025 with a new series of delves I've been wanting to do for some time, building out an ML application using modern microservices principles. In this...

items.title: Delve 8: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 3, The Business Logic and Interface Layers
items.content_html: ML Microservices, Completing the Layers Hello data delvers! In part two of this series we refactored to create a separate data layer of our application. As a refresher, these are the three layers of the application we are creating:

items.title: Delve 9: Migrating from Jekyll to Material for MkDocs
items.content_html: From one Static Site to Another Greetings data delvers! The sharper-eyed among you may have noticed that the website looks a little bit different now. No you aren't seeing things. I recently completely changed the backend of the site from Jekyll to Material for MkDocs. The process was overall pretty smooth but had...

items.title: Delve 21: A Local Claude Code
items.content_html: Hello data delvers! Though I am cautiously skeptical of the hype around AI, one area that I have seen my own productivity increase is by leveraging AI as a pair programmer. Up until this point, I have primarily relied on Github Copilot as my AI assistant. However, I recently gave Claude Code a try at work and was...

items.title: Delve 0: Hello Labyrinth (World)!
items.content_html: Entering the Labyrinth Welcome to my blog data delver! I'm so glad you found your way here! If you're like me, when you first started out with data science and machine learning, you may have been feeling overwhelmed. With so many different concepts to learn it may have seemed as if there was an insurmountable...

items.title: Delve 1: The (Hidden) Danger of Notebooks in Production
items.content_html: When Good Intentions go Awry At many points in my career I have come across the topic of deploying code related to machine learning models in the form of Jupyter Notebooks. Often, the push towards this idea comes from a place of good intentions, of speeding up the the model deployment process or enabling better access...

items.title: Delve 2: My Recommended Machine Learning & Data Science Resources!
items.content_html: My Go To List of Machine Learning & Data Science Resources I have often been asked what resources I recommend for those looking to get into machine learning, whether you want to be a data scientist or ml engineer. In this delve I'll cover my go to list of resources I continue to rely on whenever I need to refresh my...

items.title: Delve 3: Fun Linux Utilities
items.content_html: *nix the Workhorse of MLOps Welcome to 2024 data delvers! I hope you had a wonderful holiday season! As we enter into the new year I'd like wanted to take some time to talk about things that make my day to day as a developer fun! As I hope to get into in future delves, for many reasons I prefer a \nix (Unix or Linux)...

items.title: Delve 5: The Quest for a Full Screen Raspberry Pi Application
items.content_html: Full Screen Applications on the Raspberry Pi, Why so Hard? Hello data delvers! Apologies for the lack of updates, life has been busy! For today I have a quick delve on a frustrating problem I had to solve, longer delves are on the way! Like I'm sure many of you, I greatly enjoy doing side projects on the Raspberry Pi...

items.title: Delve 7: Let's Build a Modern ML Microservice Application - Part 2, The Data Layer
items.content_html: ML Microservices, the Second Hello data delvers! In part one of this series we left off creating a basic application that allowed us to search for a work by title in the Metropolitan Museum of Art's collection. We were able set up a basic project structure as well as the tooling we would need to get the project off...

items.title: Delve 14: Reflections on a Job Quest
items.content_html: A New Chapter Hello data delvers! I recently successfully wrapped up a journey to find a new job! Along the way, I had the opportunity to interview at several different companies, experience many different styles of interviews, and explore different types of roles. For this delve, I intend to distill some thoughts...
